scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Strong rule extraction (8 rules with individual confidence ratings, exceeding the 5 minimum) and excellent clarifying questions (7 critical unknowns surfaced, exceeding the 5 minimum). 'Demand' is clearly identified as undefined (Unknown #1 and throughout). However, two critical pass criteria are violated: (1) confidence.level is 65, which exceeds the pass threshold of <=40 and hits the fail threshold of >=65; (2) Given/When/Then testable assertions were written for nearly every rule despite the rubric requiring none. The output also includes a full business impact analysis and ubiquitous language glossary, treating the input as more specified than it is. Saved from a fail score by the exceptional question surfacing, explicit ambiguity flagging, and the bottom-line statement that 'no team should start building until unknowns are resolved.'

observations.text: 8 business rules extracted, each with individual confidence ratings — exceeds the 5-rule minimum substantially
7 critical unknowns surfaced as clarifying questions, all highly relevant and well-reasoned
'Demand' explicitly called out as undefined in Unknown #1 and flagged throughout the analysis
Bottom-line statement correctly warns against building before resolving key unknowns
No specific numeric values invented (no '20% increase when demand exceeds 80%' type fabrications)
confidence.level of 65 is far too high for this level of ambiguity — rubric requires <=40 for pass, and 65 hits the fail threshold
Given/When/Then testable assertions written for Rules 1-8, directly violating the 'no Given/When/Then criteria' pass requirement
Full business impact analysis with stakeholder mapping treats the vague CEO statement as more specified than it is
Several rules rated HIGH confidence (Rules 1, 3, 8) when the vague input warrants medium at best for most
Ubiquitous language section defines terms like 'Baseline Price' that were never mentioned in the input, implying more domain clarity than exists

agent_output_excerpt:
# DynaPricing — Domain Rule Extraction

## Domain Analysis

```yaml
domain_analysis:
  business_context: >
    Real-time dynamic pricing for delivery services based on demand signals.
    The system adjusts prices automatically to balance competitiveness and profitability.
  ubiquitous_language:
    - term: DynaPricing
      definition: The dynamic pricing system that adjusts delivery prices in real time based on demand.
    - term: Demand
      definition: A measure of current delivery request
